architecture.summary: N-best T5 is a fine-tuned T5 sequence-to-sequence model for ASR error correction. The encoder processes concatenated N-best ASR hypotheses (prefixed with 'text correction:' and separated by special tokens) instead of a single 1-best hypothesis. The decoder generates corrected transcriptions. The system integrates ASR N-best lists or lattices into the generation space via constrained decoding algorithms that restrict outputs to paths with high ASR probability, combining ASR scores with language model scores via interpolation.
trained_components: interpolation weight λ for score combination

biblio.title: N-best T5: Robust ASR Error Correction using Multiple Input Hypotheses and Constrained Decoding Space

claims.statement: Using 10-best T5 with unconstrained decoding achieves 7.2% WER reduction compared to the baseline ASR model on test sets
Using 10-best T5 with lattice-constrained decoding achieves up to 12% WER reduction on the test sets
Oracle WER in 10-best list improves by 42.8% compared to ASR baseline on test sets
Using 5-best T5 achieves 4.7% WER reduction compared to baseline ASR model
Lattice-constrained decoding yields slightly better performance than N-best constrained decoding because a lattice contains more possible paths compared to an N-best list and yields lower oracle WER
1-best T5 fails to yield good results only using the 1-best decoding hypothesis as model input
The model can be applied to black-box ASR models integrated in cloud services since N-best lists can be easily obtained from ASR systems without the need of in-depth access
Constrained decoding with interpolation weight λ around 0.75 outperforms plain beam search
